diagrams.title: Coset Partition of a Group
diagrams.description: Shows how left cosets partition a finite group G into disjoint subsets of equal size
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how Lagrange's Theorem is proved - counting elements via coset partition

diagrams.title: Subgroup Lattice of Z_12
diagrams.description: Hierarchical diagram showing inclusion relationships between all subgroups of cyclic group Z_12
diagrams.purpose: See correspondence between divisors of 12 and subgroup orders - one subgroup per divisor

diagrams.title: Lagrange's Theorem Relationship
diagrams.description: Visual representation of the equation |G| = [G:H] × |H| showing the relationship between group order, subgroup order, and index
elements: Divided into equal blocks, each block = |H| elements
diagrams.purpose: Understand the fundamental equation and how to compute any of the three quantities given the other two

diagrams.title: Order of Element in Cyclic Group
diagrams.description: Shows geometric interpretation of element order in cyclic group as rotational symmetry
elements: Element of order n = rotation by 360°/n
Generator = rotation by smallest angle that returns to start after full rotations
Each element's order = angle that divides 360°
diagrams.purpose: Intuitive understanding of order concept as divisibility in cyclic groups
